description: Gene Set Enrichment Analysis. If you are using GSEA on RNA-seq data, please read these guidelines.
author: Aravind Subramanian, Pablo Tamayo, David Eby; Broad Institute
plan: ## GSEA GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan ### 1. Overview GSEA is a GenePattern module that Gene Set Enrichment Analysis. If you are using GSEA on RNA-seq data, please read these guidelines. ### 2. Parameters The module exposes 30 parameters: - Required: expression.dataset, gene.sets.database, number.of.permutations, phenotype.labels, collapse.dataset, permutation.type, scoring.scheme, metric.for.ranking.genes, gene.list.sorting.mode, gene.list.ordering.mode, max.gene.set.size, min.gene.set.size, collapsing.mode.for.probe.sets.with.more.than.one.match, normalization.mode, randomization.mode, omit.features.with.no.symbol.match, make.detailed.gene.set.report, median.for.class.metrics, number.of.markers, plot.graphs.for.the.top.sets.of.each.phenotype, random.seed, save.random.ranked.lists, create.svgs, create.gcts, dev.mode - Optional: target.profile, chip.platform.file, selected.gene.sets, output.file.name, alt.delim ### 3. Command Line ``` /opt/gsea/GSEA_4.3.2/gsea-cli.sh GSEA -res -cls -collapse -mode -norm -nperm -permute -rnd_type -scoring_scheme -metric -sort -order -include_only_symbols -make_sets -median -num -plot_top_x -rnd_seed -save_rnd_lists -set_max -set_min -gmx_list -create_svgs -create_gcts -target_profile -dev_mode -zip_report true -run_as_genepattern true ``` ### 4. Docker Image Docker image: genepattern/gsea_4.3.2\:0.1 ### 5. Validation Strategy - Validate all required parameters are provided - Validate file formats match expected extensions - Check parameter value ranges where applicable ### 6. Implementation Roadmap Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation Phase 2: Docker container setup Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: expression.dataset
parameters.description: Dataset file - .res, .gct

parameters.name: gene.sets.database
parameters.description: Gene sets database from GSEA website. Upload a gene set if your gene set is not listed as a choice from MSigDB.

parameters.name: number.of.permutations
parameters.description: Number of permutations to perform

parameters.name: phenotype.labels
parameters.description: Cls file - .cls, must be binary

parameters.name: target.profile
parameters.description: Name of the target phenotype profile.  Only applicable if class file defines continuous labels (one or more phenotype profiles).  Leave blank if class file defines a discrete phenotype.

parameters.name: collapse.dataset
parameters.description: Select whether to collapse each probe set in the expression dataset into a single vector for the gene, which gets identified by its gene symbol.  It is also possible to remap symbols from one namespace to another without collapsing (an error will occur if multiple source genes map to a single destination gene).  No_Collapse will use the dataset as-is.

parameters.name: permutation.type
parameters.description: Type of permutations to perform

parameters.name: chip.platform.file
parameters.description: DNA Chip (array) annotation file from GSEA website.  Upload your own chip file if the one corresponding to your DNA Microarray platform is not listed in the drop-down menu.  A chip file is only required if collapse dataset is set to true.

parameters.name: scoring.scheme
parameters.description: The statistic used to score hits (gene set members) and misses (non-members)

parameters.name: metric.for.ranking.genes
parameters.description: Class separation metric - gene markers are ranked using this metric to produce the gene list

parameters.name: gene.list.sorting.mode
parameters.description: Mode in which scores from the gene list should be considered

parameters.name: gene.list.ordering.mode
parameters.description: Direction in which the gene list should be ordered

parameters.name: max.gene.set.size
parameters.description: Gene sets larger than this are excluded from the analysis

parameters.name: min.gene.set.size
parameters.description: Gene sets smaller than this are excluded from the analysis

parameters.name: collapsing.mode.for.probe.sets.with.more.than.one.match
parameters.description: Collapsing mode.  This has no effect when remapping symbols.

parameters.name: normalization.mode
parameters.description: Normalization mode

parameters.name: randomization.mode
parameters.description: Type of phenotype randomization (does not apply to gene set permutations)

parameters.name: omit.features.with.no.symbol.match
parameters.description: If there is no known gene symbol match for a probe set, omit it from the collapsed dataset

parameters.name: make.detailed.gene.set.report
parameters.description: Create detailed gene set report (heat map, mountain plot, etc.) for every enriched gene set

parameters.name: median.for.class.metrics
parameters.description: Use the median of each class instead of the mean for the class separation metrics

parameters.name: number.of.markers
parameters.description: Number of markers

parameters.name: plot.graphs.for.the.top.sets.of.each.phenotype
parameters.description: Plot GSEA mountain and related plots for the top sets of each phenotype

parameters.name: random.seed
parameters.description: Seed to use for randomization

parameters.name: save.random.ranked.lists
parameters.description: Whether to save random ranked lists

parameters.name: create.svgs
parameters.description: Whether to create SVG images (compressed) along with PNGs

parameters.name: selected.gene.sets
parameters.description: Semicolon-separated list of gene sets from the provided gene sets database files (GMT/GMX/GRP).  If you are using multiple files then you *must* prefix each selected gene set with its file name followed by '\#' (like "my_file1.gmt\#selected_gene_set1,my_file2.gmt\#selected_gene_set2").  With a single file only the names are necessary.  Leave this blank to select all gene sets.

parameters.name: output.file.name
parameters.description: Name of the output ZIP file.

parameters.name: alt.delim
parameters.description: Optional alternate delimiter character for gene set names instead of comma for use with selected.gene.sets.  If used, a semicolon is recommended.

parameters.name: create.gcts
parameters.description: Whether to save the dataset subsets backing the GSEA report heatmaps as GCT files; these will be subsets of your original dataset corresponding only to the genes of the heatmap.

parameters.name: dev.mode
parameters.description: Enable developer mode.
